threads: Пятьсот лет назад в туманности Ориона столкнулись две (или больше) протозвёзды. Удар породил веер из молекулярных струй, которые до сих пор летят со скоростью 350 км/с — и, словно галактики после Большого взрыва, ускоряются по мере удаления от центра. Энергию дала гравитация. Такие космические ДТП случаются раз в 140 лет и, возможно, объясняют, как зажигаются самые массивные звёзды.

title: Dance Without a Choreographer: How Graphene Separated Space and Time
oneliner: For the first time, physicists independently measured the spatial and temporal exponents of a quantum transition, discovering that screening changes only the rhythm of electrons, leaving their dance unchanged.
description: The quantum Hall effect in graphene kept a secret for decades: why does the spatial entanglement exponent γ≈2.4 stubbornly resist theory? An international team used graphene 'sandwiches' with two gates to geometrically control screening. As the gate distance shrank, the fluctuation rhythm doubled —
abstract: In phase transitions of the quantum Hall effect, Coulomb interactions complicate the critical behavior, mixing spatial and dynamic effects. Scientists developed a method to separately measure the dynamic exponent z and the localization length exponent γ: they combined temperature and current scaling in graphene devices with a double graphite gate, varying the screening radius by the ratio of magnetic length to gate distance. It turned out that screening increases z from ≈1 to ≈2, while γ remains around 2.4 — much like a tone knob changes the sound color but not the volume. This paves the way to purposefully separate the influence of disorder and interactions in a wide class of two-dimensional systems.
threads: Оказывается, в квантовом мире пространство и время можно настраивать порознь. Физики научились менять темп электронного танца, не касаясь его фигур — словно хореограф ускоряет ритм, сохраняя па. Такой контроль открывает путь к точной инженерии квантовых материалов и, возможно, к созданию «реостатов» для самой реальности.

title: A Day in the Quantum Beat: How Cesium Atoms Became an Eternal Metronome
oneliner: Chinese physicists turned a cloud of cesium atoms into a continuous time crystal that spontaneously pulsed for a record-breaking 16.95 hours.
description: In the experiment, Rydberg atoms excited by three lasers and modulated by a radio-frequency field produced stable, undamped oscillations. The key to the record was suppressing dissipation so effectively that the real part of the Liouvillian spectrum—a damping indicator—practically vanished, allowing
abstract: Time crystals are quantum systems that spontaneously maintain rhythmic oscillations without external interference. In a new study with Rydberg atoms (extremely sensitive excited atoms), researchers managed to suppress destructive effects and extend oscillations to a record 16.95 hours. The secret lies in 'closing the Liouvillian gap' (the vanishing of a gap in the evolution operator's spectrum), akin to a perfectly balanced feedback system where losses are immediately compensated.
threads: Ритм, не стихающий 17 часов — дольше светового дня. Этот квантовый метроном бьётся, словно сердце, нашедшее резонанс с самим пульсом космоса. Мы учимся создавать память, которая не забывает, и сенсоры, чувствующие дыхание невидимых миров.

title: Ghost Garden: How Neutrino Showers Sprout Dark Matter Seeds
oneliner: Primordial black holes, once thought frozen in the fire of the early universe, suddenly began to grow on an invisible neutrino 'soup' — and this flips our understanding of dark matter on its head.
description: It was long believed that in the boiling cauldron after the Big Bang, primordial black holes were doomed to eternal hunger: their gravitational radius is so tiny that radiation sweeps by without stopping. But new research reveals a secret: neutrinos, ghostly wanderers capable of piercing a light-yea
abstract: Physicists have refined our understanding of how primordial black holes grew in the hot plasma of the early universe, now accounting for the absorption of neutrinos — those almost ghostly particles that, as it turns out, play a key role. Calculations show that intermediate-mass and supermassive black holes gained weight much faster than previously thought, shifting important peaks in the thermal history of matter. This revises the estimate of how much dark matter such objects could make up. A surprising takeaway: intermediate-mass black holes may be widespread, not rare exotica.
threads: Нейтрино — космические фантомы, проходящие сквозь световой год свинца как сквозь туман. Но в кипящем котле ранней Вселенной их незримый ливень был столь густ, что утолял голод первичных чёрных дыр, превращая их из песчинок в горы. И возможно, именно эти древние исполины — та самая тёмная материя, что прячет лицо мироздания.

title: Warm Start for a Quantum Engine: How Machine Learning Accelerates Molecular Calculations
oneliner: Element-specific ML models slashed the number of self-consistency iterations in DFTB by 84% — from organic molecules to solid electrolytes.
description: In semi-empirical DFTB, self-consistency of charges often stumbles, especially in polarized systems. Researchers from the Fritz Haber Institute applied machine learning to predict nearly converged atomic charges directly from structure — a ‘warm start’ for the iterative algorithm. Models based on ke
abstract: Scientists have accelerated quantum chemical calculations using the DFTB method, which often slows down when searching for a self-consistent charge distribution. With machine learning, a model predicts optimal initial charges for each type of atom, using the SOAP descriptor (smooth overlap of atomic positions) and kernel ridge regression. It's like giving a navigator not a random route but an almost correct one — iterations converge faster. The method was tested on organics, biomolecules, metal oxides, and solid electrolytes, showing consistent speedup.
threads: Атомные заряды — как настроечные колки в молекулярном оркестре. Без верной инициализации самосогласование тянет бесконечную какофонию. ML учит каждый элемент играть с листа: для оксидов никеля время «настройки» сократилось на 84%. Это шаг к тому, чтобы симуляции материалов стали почти мгновенными, превратив дизайн катализаторов и батарей в рутинную задачу.

title: The Invisible Conductor of the Gravitational Symphony
oneliner: Neglecting overtones distorts masses and distances, but the scalar charge remains clear — like a voice that cannot be silenced.
description: The LISA observatory will listen for years to the gravitational symphony of black hole mergers. The study revealed: if the subtle "overtones" — post-adiabatic corrections — are omitted, mass estimates drift sharply, but the scalar charge, a hypothetical echo of new fields, remains remarkably stable.
abstract: Scientists have crafted an upgraded model of gravitational waves for the upcoming LISA detector, factoring in hypothetical scalar fields and ultra-subtle corrections to black hole motion. It turns out that ignoring these tweaks skews the measured properties of the holes, but one key parameter — the scalar charge — holds rock-solid. However, trying to fit such objects with Einstein's standard theory leads to false conclusions. A twist: even without scalar fields, the spin of a lightweight black hole is almost undetectable, defying expectations.
threads: 100 тысяч витков — словно граммофонная пластинка, на которой медленно проступает скрытая дорожка. Игнорируя обертоны, мы теряем детали оркестровки, но основной мотив остаётся чистым. Скалярный заряд чёрных дыр — это тихий шёпот новых измерений; LISA сможет его уловить даже в несовершенной записи. Когда-нибудь так мы настроимся на тёмную энергию, как на забытую радиостанцию.

title: The Rocket Effect of Domain Walls: Mass Asymmetry Propels Vacuums
oneliner: Differences in scalar field excitation masses turn domain walls into microscopic rockets: anisotropic thrust destroys the cosmological defect network.
description: A new study demonstrates: if the masses of a scalar field in neighboring vacuums are unequal, domain walls — the boundaries between vacuum states — emit asymmetric radiation. A "rocket effect" arises: the recoil force causes the wall to drift towards the heavier vacuum, expanding the lighter one. Nu
abstract: Domain walls are boundaries between regions of space where a physical field settled into different vacuum states. If the mass of the field's particles depends on the vacuum, the radiation from the walls becomes anisotropic: it streams mainly toward the side with the lower mass. This effect, similar to jet propulsion, pushes the walls toward the lighter vacuum, speeding up the decay of the entire cosmological network of walls. It was previously thought that potential barrier asymmetry dominated, but new research shows that the difference in particle masses plays the main role. For non-degenerate vacua, this mechanism adds an extra dynamical shift that can either accelerate or slow down the walls' decay.
threads: Невидимые плёнки толщиной с атомное ядро могли бы разорвать Вселенную. Но природа включила ракетный двигатель: если массы квантов по разные стороны отличаются, стенка испускает асимметричное излучение и дрейфует, как ракета, расширяя лёгкий вакуум и уничтожая сеть. Гравитационные волны от этих катаклизмов донесут до нас информацию о физике за пределами Стандартной модели.

title: The Hidden Wind of Neutron Stars: How Neutrons Propel Magnetic Vortices
oneliner: Taking into account the Fermi liquid coupling of neutrons and protons reveals an additional longitudinal force acting on proton vortices in superconducting neutron stars — like a tailwind for magnetic surfers.
description: In the cores of neutron stars, protons become superconductors, generating quantum vortices with magnetic flux. Previously, it was thought that the force on vortices comes only from electron scattering. New research shows that the neutron normal component also creates a longitudinal force via Fermi l
abstract: In neutron stars, aside from the known force from electrons, vortices of superfluid protons experience a longitudinal push from neutrons. This effect arises from Fermi-liquid interactions (a quantum collective interaction of particles) and is described by an equation where the force is proportional to the relative velocity. Unlike the electron force, it does not deflect the vortex sideways. Imagine neutrons 'shoving' the vortex like a water current pushing a turbine blade, but without any lateral shift. Estimates show this force matters in neutron star dynamics — a key step toward accurate pulsar models.
threads: Представьте протонные вихри сёрферами на магнитных волнах. Раньше считалось, что их толкают только электроны. Но теперь открылся попутный ветер — нейтроны дуют в спину. Эта тихая сила может объяснить загадочные вспышки магнитаров и дрожь пульсаров. Кто знает, куда заведёт нас эта космическая гидродинамика?
